statement: App-wide skeleton loading states — Every app surface that loads data or async content (Builder shell/stage/cards, Home, Version pages, modals, lists, editor panels) must render a proper React skeleton/placeholder that matches the final layout during load, instead of spinners, layout shift, or blank states. This complements REQ-VR-003 (Builder progressive load sequence, backend-scoped ordering) but is broader: it applies app-wide and mandates skeleton placeholders as the loading affordance. Skeletons must respect prefers-reduced-motion (no infinite shimmer when reduced) per core.md §20.
